rulings.Case Title: INVESTEK PROPERTIES VS ALTA MESA WIND
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 30, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiffs INVESTEK PROPERTIES COMPANY LLC'S and INVESTEK PROPERTIES COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Further Responses Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issue the following tentative ruling: Motion denied in part as moot, except motion is granted in part as to those discovery requests that could be construed as seeking information about royalties. Sanctions denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to cgeerhart@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARCIA SANCHEZ et al VS. WILLIAM RALPH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Diamond Heights Village Association'S Motion To Compel Physical Exam On Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 30, 2013, Line 10, Defendant Diamond Heights Village Association'S Motion To Compel Physical Exam On Plaintiff. Plaintiff Sanchez is ordered to appear for a physical examination at the Embassy Suites, San Francisco Airport, 250 Gateway Blvd., South San Francisco, CA 94080. The parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding the time and date of the examination. The examination shall occur by October 15, 2013. The entire examination shall last not longer than two hours and will be conducted by Andrew Saxon, M.D. The examination will be a general physical examination and spirometry to be done in a painless, passive manner. A more focused detailed examination may be conducted following the general examination to better identify the source of any specific problems which may include: A spirometry after the inhalation of a bronchodilator if the at rest breathing shows evidence of an obstructive abnormality; this test involves the inhalation of two breaths of medication, which is designed to relieve the obstruction followed by performance of the breathing test in 20 minutes. The Plaintiff is ordered to cooperate during the examination so that it may be completed within the two hour limitation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: MARCIA SANCHEZ et al VS. WILLIAM RALPH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 30, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT WILLIAM RALPH's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is denied. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Defendant's motion is timely. (See CCP §§ 437c(a); 12a.) Defendant shifted the burden by producing prima facie evidence that he did not act with malice, oppression, or fraud as contemplated under Civ. Code. § 3294. Defendant produces evidence that he remained in communication with Plaintiffs as the leaks were occurring, and that he spent over $1000 and hundreds of hours attempting resolve the plumbing issues. (Declaration of Ralph in Support of Motion for Summary Adjudication, 8-10) Also, Defendant has produced evidence that there is no way he could have placed the brown bag of liquid in the space between his unit's floor and Plaintiffs' unit's ceiling. (Declaration of Hunter in Support of Motion for Summary Adjudication, 7.) Consequently, the burden shifts to Plaintiffs. Plaintiffs point to several pieces of evidence showing that create a triable issue of fact as to whether Defendant engaged in fraudulent behavior. Plaintiff points to Defendant's statements denying that water had migrated from his unit to Plaintiffs' apartment, and that he had never received a letter from his insurance company on October 21, 2012 requesting that he take responsibility for the leak. (See Evidence cited in Plaintiffs' Separate Statement of Facts in Opposition to Motion ("PSSF"), Nos. 18, 21.) The evidence shows Defendant later admits these statements are incorrect. This is sufficient evidence to create a triable issue of material fact as to whether Plaintiff has shown by clear and convincing evidence that Defendant acted fraudulently regarding his role in Plaintiffs' injury. (Civ. Code §3294.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 30, 2013, Line 13, CROSS COMPLAINANT MICHAEL BEESON, AN INDIVIDUAL's Motion To Compel Supplemental Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions. Defendant Beeson's Motion to Compel Supplemental Responses to Special Interrogatories, One, Two, and Three is granted with full and complete responses to be served within 30 days from the date of this order. To the extent that Plaintiff has any documents referred to in Special Interrogatories Two and Three, Plaintiff is ordered to provide the information requested by those interrogatories. Monetary sanctions are awarded in favor of Defendant Beeson and against Plaintiff and her counsel in the amount of $2,500.00 to be paid within 30 days of the hearing. The court will not provide a court reporter for this hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. rulings.Case Title: SHAWN CHOY et al VS. CENTURY SURETY COMPANY, AN OHIO CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 30, 2013, Line 19, PLAINTIFFs SHAWN CHOY and KAYLA CHA's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanction (Part 1 of 2). The Court finds as follows regarding Plaintiff's RPD Set 1: RPD 16: Century says it has provided all responsive writings. The Mayer Decl., 8, suggests that there may be additional training materials used in presentations, even if written materials are not distributed. To the extent any additional training materials used in California training sessions exist, they should be produced. RPD 17: Century says it has provided all responsive writings. To the extent any additional materials showing changes in claims handling guidelines in California exist, they should be produced. RPD 18: Even if the adjusters who handled this claim were attorneys, the materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of Century's standards for claim handling. Century is ordered to produce responsive documents, if any exist. RPD 19: Even if the adjusters who handled this claim were attorneys, the materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of Century's pattern of compliance with insurance regulations. Century is ordered to produce responsive documents limited to period 2008-2011, if any exist. RPD 20: Even if the adjusters who handled this claim were attorneys, the materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of Century's pattern of compliance with insurance regulations. Century is ordered to produce responsive documents limited to period 2008-2011, if any exist. RPD 21: The materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of Century's pattern of compliance with insurance regulations. Century is ordered to produce responsive documents limited to period 2008-2011, if any exist. RPD 22: The materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of Century's pattern of compliance with insurance regulations. Century is ordered to produce responsive documents limited to period 2008-2011, if any exist. RPD 23: The materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of Century's pattern of compliance with insurance regulations. Century is ordered to produce responsive documents limited to period 2008-2011, if any exist. RPD 24: The materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of Century's pattern of compliance with insurance regulations. Century is ordered to produce responsive documents limited to period 2008-2011, if any exist. RPD 28: Even if the adjusters who handled this claim were attorneys, they may have received some training, and the materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of Century's standards for claim handling. Century is ordered to produce responsive documents, if any exist. (End of Part 1; See Part 2.) =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: SHAWN CHOY et al VS. CENTURY SURETY COMPANY, AN OHIO CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Shawn Choy And Kayla Cha'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanction (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 30, 2013, Line 19, PLAINTIFFs SHAWN CHOY and KAYLA CHA's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanction (Part 2 of 2). RPD 30: Century says it has provided all responsive writings. The Mayer Decl., 8, suggests that there may be additional training materials used in presentations, even if written materials are not distributed. To the extent any additional training materials used in California training sessions exist, they should be produced. RPD 45: The materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of Century's claims handling practices in case involving 3rd party assaults. Century is ordered to produce coverage and acceptance letters for California claims limited to the period from 2008-2011. Names in these letters may be redacted to protect 3rd party privacy. RPD 46: The materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of how Century construed its policy language. If responsive documents exist, they should be produced. Request for sanctions is denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of Part 2.) =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL GIEDGOWD VS. COAST NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY, A BUSINESS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 30, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT COAST NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is denied. Defendant fails to shift the burden or production. The facts show that there is a disputed issue of material fact regarding whether defendant breached the contract and/or acted in bad faith. There is no evidence that defendant made an effort to resolve all of the claims and the trier of fact will determine whether defendant was reasonable when it deferred to the criminal attorney's instructions not to contact the injured parties and compromise the claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)
